data.title: Northpointe Bancshares, Inc. GAAP EPS of $0.62 misses by $0.01, revenue of $63.4M misses by $0.36M
data.description:
• Northpointe Bancshares, Inc. press release (NPB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.62 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $63.4M (+18.9% Y/Y) misses by $0.36M.

data.title: Weatherford International GAAP EPS of $1.49, revenue of $1.15M
data.description:
• Weatherford International press release (WFRD): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.49.
  • Revenue of $1.15M.
  • First quarter operating income of $123 million decreased 13% year-over-year
  • First quarter net income of $108 million increased 42% year-over-year; net income margin of 9.4%
  • First quarter adjusted EBITDA* of $233 million, decreased 8% year-over-year; adjusted EBITDA margin* of 20.2% decreased 98 basis points year-over-year
  • First quarter cash provided by operating activities of $136 million and adjusted free cash flow* of $85 million
  • Shareholder return of $30 million for the quarter, which included dividend payments of $20 million and share repurchases of $10 million

data.title: Voyager Therapeutics CFO resigns; Robin Swartz takes financial charge
data.description:
• Voyager Therapeutics (VYGR) announced on Tuesday in an SEC Form 8-K filing that its CFO, Nathan Jorgensen, has resigned, effective May 8, 2026, to take up a new opportunity.
  • The Board has appointed Robin Swartz (current COO & CBO) as the new Principal Financial Officer and Treasurer, effective immediately upon transition, ensuring continuity in finance leadership.
  • Adding to the leadership reshuffle, Amy Quinlan has been named Principal Accounting Officer, strengthening the company’s accounting oversight.
  • Following the news, the stock closed ~2.60% lower at ~$4.12.

data.title: Prudential Financial extends its Japan voluntary sales suspension
data.description:
Prudential Financial (PRU)  will extend its voluntary suspension of new sales activity at Prudential of Japan ((POJ)) by an additional 180 days, it said Tuesday, adding that the scope and complexity of the required changes within POJ are greater than previously anticipated.

The extension follows a 90-day suspension that started on Feb. 9, 2026. The company said it will take more time to design and implement operational, governance, organizational, and related changes necessary to resume sales at POJ. The original suspension was conducted to address previously disclosed employee misconduct, including some employees making inappropriate investment solicitations, it said at the time.

Prudential Financial (PRU) also started an independent, third-party review of POJ's management system earlier this year as part of its governance process, which is expected to take several months to complete.

Prudential of Japan remains financially sound and able to meet its obligations, the company said. Japan continues to be a core component of Prudential Financial’s global footprint, and the parent company is providing full support and resources as these reforms advance.

The voluntary suspension does not apply to Prudential Financial’s or PGIM’s other business units in Japan, including Gibraltar Life and Prudential Gibraltar Financial Life.

Prudential Financial (PRU) will hold a conference call  at 5:30 PM ET.

data.title: Charlton Aria gets Nasdaq notice over delayed annual filing
data.description:
• Charlton Aria (CHARU) on Tuesday said it has received a notice from Nasdaq for failing to timely file its annual report.
  • The company said it has not yet filed its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, putting it out of compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • Charlton Aria said the notice has no immediate impact on the listing or trading of its securities.
  • The company has until June 15, 2026 to file the report or submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • If Nasdaq accepts the plan, it may grant up to 180 days from the original due date to regain compliance.
  • The company said it intends to file the Form 10-K as soon as possible.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tanker firms DHT, Frontline, Nordic American downgraded at Evercore ISI
data.description:
DHT Holdings (DHT) and Frontline (FRO) were downgraded at Evercore ISI on Tuesday to In-Line from Outperform, while Nordic American Tanker (NAT) was cut to Underperform from In-Line, noting spot rates reside at record levels, but "the parabolic move over the last two months has been driven by an anomalous factor, namely the closing of the Strait of Hormuz."

As investors consider the next move in rates, asset values, and earnings, "a thesis regarding an eventual resolution to Iranian hostilities points to an unfavorable rate of change, with only 'when' and 'how much' in question," Evercore analyst Jonathan Chappell wrote.

The lack of follow-through in tanker stocks since the war began and rates spiked "highlights a reluctance to further push premiums, and line of sight on reversion is likely to lead to profit-taking," the analyst said.

VLCC orders set a record in Q1, but Chappell sees demand growth threatened by higher oil prices, rendering greater potential downside risk to utilization when the Middle East war ends.

ETFs: (BWET), (BOAT)

data.title: Chubb stock sinks as Q1 GAAP EPS misses estimates amid rise in realized losses
data.description:
Chubb (CB) was trading lower as Q1 GAAP EPS fell short of the average analyst estimate, while net realized losses jumped. 

Shares were 2.21% down to $322.00 on Tuesday after market hours.

GAAP EPS of $5.88 rose from $3.29 in the same period of 2025, but was below the consensus of $6.71.

"Strong growth in P&C underwriting income, investment income, and life income led to core operating earnings of $2.7B, or $6.82 per share, both up more than 80% over the prior year's first quarter, which was impacted by the California wildfires," said CEO Evan Greenberg.

"Excluding CATs, core operating income was up 10.7% and EPS was up 13.5%," said Greenberg.

Net premiums written increased 10.7% year-over-year to $14.01B.

The P&C segment saw an increase of 7.2% in the net premiums written to $11.72B, while Global P&C (excluding Agriculture) recorded a 7.1% rise to $11.41B. Life Insurance reported a 33.1% growth to $2.29B.

Net premiums earned rose to $13.46B from $12.00B.

Net realized losses were up to $407M from $116M a year ago.

Earnings call is scheduled for tomorrow at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: WesBanco Non-GAAP EPS of $0.91 beats by $0.05, revenue of $257M misses by $7.54M
data.description:

data.title: Capital One Q1 earnings rise less than expected amid Discover integration
data.description:
Capital One Financial (COF) stock slid 3.1% in Tuesday after-hours trading as Q1 earnings and revenue came in lighter than the Wall Street consensus estimates. The credit card lender continued to integrate Discover's operations into its business.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $4.42, trailing the average analyst estimate of $4.57, rose from $3.86 in Q4 2025 and $4.06 in Q1 2025.

Total revenue of $15.2B, missing the $15.4B consensus, decreased from $15.6B in the prior quarter and climbed from $10.0B a year ago.

"Our results in the first quarter reflect solid top-line growth and strong credit performance," said founder, Chairman, and CEO Richard D. Fairbank. "The Discover integration continues to go well, and we continue to build momentum from this game-changing acquisition." The acquisition closed in May 2025.

Net interest income of $12.1B, lagging the $12.3B consensus, dropped from $12.5B in Q4 and increased from $8.01B in last year’s Q1. Its net interest margin of 7.87% narrowed from 8.26% in the previous quarter and widened from 6.93% a year ago.

Q4 pretax preprovision earnings of $6.77B, vs. the consensus of $7.08B, increased from $6.24B in Q4 and from $4.10B in the prior year’s Q1.

Provision for credit losses of $4.07B, vs. the consensus estimate of $3.70B, fell from $4.14B in the previous quarter and $2.37B a year ago.

The net charge-off rate of 3.45% was unchanged from Q4 and edged up from 3.40% in last year’s Q1.

Noninterest expense totaled $8.46B, down from $9.34B in the prior quarter and up from $5.90B a year ago.

Total deposits of $489.1B at Dec. 31, 2025, increased from $475.8B at Dec. 31, 2025. Loans held for investment were $447.8B at March 31 vs. $453.6B at Dec. 31.

Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

data.title: Atlantic American receives Nasdaq notice over delayed annual filing
data.description:
• Atlantic American (AAME) on Tuesday said it has received a notice from Nasdaq stating it is not in compliance with listing rules due to a delay in filing its annual report.
  • The company said it has not yet filed its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, breaching Nasdaq requirements for timely financial reporting.
  • Atlantic American said the notice has no immediate impact on the listing or trading of its shares.
  • The company has 60 days to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq may grant up to 180 days from the original filing deadline for the company to file the report.

  • Atlantic American said it is working to complete and file the Form 10-K as soon as practicable.

  • AAME -0.60% after hours to $2.45.
  • Source: Press Release
